name: Wheel of Heaven Translation Glossary
description: Lexical decisions made by the Wheel of Heaven Translation Program. Distinct from content/i18n/glossary.json, which records site-UI terminology. Each entry records a source-language term, the standard PD-translation rendering, the WoH choice, the rationale, and the verses where the choice applies. Pinned to per-chapter translations via `glossaryVersion` in chapter metadata.
scopeNote:
**v2.39.0 (Genesis 40) adds three central entries from the cupbearer-and-baker dream chapter and extends four existing entries' appliesTo (scope-formalization) plus one refId-normalization.** Added: (i) `sar-ha-mashqim-sar-ha-ofim-chief-cupbearer-baker` (vv 1, 2, 5, 9, 13, 16, 20, 21, 22, 23) — the cupbearer/butler (*mashqeh*) and baker (*ofeh*) base-offices and their *sar ha-X* court-titles, parallel to the already-central `sar-ha-tabbachim`; ASV-baseline 'chief butler' / 'chief baker' retained, the literal 'cupbearer' (one-who-gives-drink) register and the Egyptian-court-office background documented; CENTRAL by cross-corpus reach (*mashqeh* at Neh 1:11; 1 Kgs 10:5). (ii) `pitron-dream-interpretation-yosef-cycle` (vv 5, 8, 12, 16, 18, 22) — the dream-cycle keyword *pitron* / verb p-t-r, near-exclusive to Gen 40-41 in the HB (cognate to the Aramaic *pesher* of Daniel); CENTRAL by the Gen 40-41 cluster; deliberately DISTINCT from `divine-dream-communication-formula` (dream-as-revelation, NOT applied to the symbolic Gen 40 dreams). (iii) `nasa-rosh-lift-up-the-head-idiom` (vv 13, 19, 20) — the chapter's keystone pun on 'lift up the head' (restoration v 13 / decapitation v 19 / both at the feast v 20); ASV-baseline rendered uniformly so the pun survives; CENTRAL as a cross-corpus idiom (the census-formula of Exod/Num; the 2 Kgs 25:27 = Jer 52:31 Jehoiachin-release). Extended appliesTo (scope-only): `saris-court-official-eunuch-ambiguity` += GEN-WOH-40:2, 40:7; `sar-ha-tabbachim-captain-of-the-guard` += GEN-WOH-40:3, 40:4; `beit-ha-sohar-prison-round-house` += GEN-WOH-40:3, 40:5; `bor-pit-cistern-dry-water-shaft` += GEN-WOH-40:15 (Yosef calls the Egyptian prison *ha-bor*; the entry's rationale already named this verse). `chesed-solitary-covenant-kindness` += GEN-WOH-40:14 (Yosef's request to the cupbearer, *ve-asita-na imadi chesed*; the entry's rationale already named this verse). refId-normalization: `ha-ivri-hebrew-etymology` GEN-40:15 → GEN-WOH-40:15 (targeted; other legacy refs left as-is, no corpus-wide sweep). Pinned to overlay `_translation-glossary.json` v1.8.0. **v2.38.0 (corrective pass, Genesis 39) adds a single bidirectional-cross-reference pointer to the existing `metzacheq-conjugal-vs-contested-disambiguation` entry (Gen 26:8): its **Cross-references** paragraph now names the Gen 39 sibling overlay entry `le-tzachek-mockery-piel-gen39` by id, completing the back-reference so the sibling pair is genuinely bidirectional (the overlay sibling already named this entry by id; the central entry previously named Gen 39:14, 17 only by verse in its polysemy-map). Pure pointer addition to an existing entry; no decision, wohChoice, rationale substance, claim_type, appliesTo, or translation-surface change. Versioned semver-minor (2.37.0 → 2.38.0) per the uniform glossary-history precedent in which every additive edit — including the v2.37.0 appliesTo-extensions and refId-normalization, the closest prior precedent for a pure pointer edit — is a minor bump; this glossary has no patch-level precedent.**

**v2.37.0 adds two Genesis 39 central entries and two appliesTo extensions + one refId-normalization, from the Yosef-in-Potifar's-house chapter.** (i) `beit-ha-sohar-prison-round-house` (vv 20-23) — the *beit ha-sohar* prison and its keeper *sar beit ha-sohar*; ASV-baseline 'prison' / 'keeper of the prison' preserved; the *sohar* 'round-house / enclosure' etymology and the Egyptian-loanword question documented without adjudication; CENTRAL on cross-cycle recurrence into Gen 40:3 + 5 (the dream-interpretation setting); distinguished from the Gen 37 *bor* and the Gen 40:15/41:14 *beit ha-bor*. (ii) `chesed-solitary-covenant-kindness` (v 21) — the SOLITARY *chesed* of *va-yet elav chased* ('inclined chesed toward him'), the companion to (not a duplicate of) the `chesed-v-emet` pair-entry; ASV-baseline 'kindness' preserved while the Glueck/Sakenfeld covenant-loyalty range is documented; CENTRAL as one of the most cross-corpus-reachable HB lemmas (~245 occurrences); appliesTo inaugurated at Gen 39:21 (first solitary occurrence in the WoH corpus). appliesTo extensions: `saris-court-official-eunuch-ambiguity` and `sar-ha-tabbachim-captain-of-the-guard` each extended from `[GEN-WOH-37:36]` to add `GEN-WOH-39:1` (Potifar's standing court-titles re-applied at the same character; scope-formalization, rationales already named Gen 39-41 as extensible). refId-normalization: `ha-ivri-hebrew-etymology` — the two Gen 39 refs in appliesTo normalized from the bare `GEN-39:14`/`GEN-39:17` to the corpus `GEN-WOH-39:14`/`GEN-WOH-39:17` form (a targeted normalization of this entry's Gen 39 refs only, not a corpus-wide sweep; the entry already documents the *Ivri* ethnonym's force at these verses). The Gen 39 *le-tzachek* mockery-Piel, the YHWH/Elohim divine-name alternation, and the *yefeh-to'ar* beauty-formula (Rachel cross-reference) are housed in the overlay (per the chapter-singular/sibling-doublet split). Pinned to overlay `_translation-glossary.json` v1.7.0 as of 2026-05-29. **v2.35.0 adds five Genesis 37 central entries from the opening of the Yosef cycle (dreams, the ketonet passim, and the sale into Mitsrayim).** (i) `sheol-underworld-realm-of-the-dead` (GEN-WOH-37:35) — the first weighty Yosef-cycle occurrence of She'ol; transliterated per the corpus loaded-term convention; underworld/grave/realm-of-the-dead semantic range documented without cosmological synthesis; cross-corpus reach across Num 16, Job, Psalms, Proverbs, Isaiah and the prophets. (ii) `ketonet-passim-ornamented-tunic-crux` (vv 3, 23, 32) — the 'coat of many colours' crux; ASV-baseline preserved on the surface while the commentary flags the philologically-stronger long-sleeved/ornamented-status-tunic readings; CENTRAL on the strength of the decisive cross-corpus 2 Sam 13:18-19 parallel (the phrase's only other HB occurrence). (iii) `saris-court-official-eunuch-ambiguity` (v 36) — Potifar's title; ASV-baseline 'officer' preserved; eunuch-vs-married-Potifar tension documented; cross-corpus reach across Gen 40, Kings, Isaiah, Jeremiah, Esther, Daniel. (iv) `sar-ha-tabbachim-captain-of-the-guard` (v 36) — 'captain of the guard' vs literal 'chief of the slaughterers'; ASV-baseline preserved; cross-corpus reach across the Yosef cycle and the Nevuzaradan rav-tabbachim parallel in Kings/Jeremiah. (v) `bor-pit-cistern-dry-water-shaft` (vv 20, 22, 24, 28, 29) — the dry-cistern of the sale-scene; ASV-baseline 'pit' preserved while the cistern-specificity (made explicit by v 24) is documented; cross-corpus reach across the Yosef cycle, the literal-cistern corpus, and the poetic pit-of-the-dead cluster. Pinned overlay `_translation-glossary.json` advances to v1.5.0 with the chapter-singular and sibling-doublet Genesis 37 entries (toledot narrative-header, the Ishmaelite/Midianite/Medanite documentary doublet, ben-zequnim, dibbatam ra'ah, na'ar, ketonet-doublet cross-refs, ba'al ha-chalomot, chayah ra'ah, the nekhot/tzeri/lot spices, haker-na recognition-motif, twenty-kesef slave-price, the Dotan toponym, and the hishtachavah obeisance lexeme). Also extends the central `puncta-extraordinaria-v9-elav` appliesTo to include GEN-WOH-37:12 (the dotted nota-accusativa over את). **v2.34.0 adds Genesis 36 — the Esau-Edom toledot, the Horite clans of Seir, and the Edomite king-list.** Five central-glossary additions: (i) `aluf-edomite-clan-chief-tribal-confederation` (~40 occurrences vv 15-43) — the technical clan-chief lexeme distinctive of the Edomite/Horite material; cross-corpus reach at Exod 15:15 (*alufei Edom*), Jer 13:21, Zech 9:7 + 12:5-6; ASV-baseline *chief* preserved; tribal-confederation pre-statehood register editorially-load-bearing. (ii) `amaleq-timna-genealogy-archetypal-enemy-origin` (v 12) — the Timna-concubine-of-Elifaz Amaleq-genealogy seeding the HB's archetypal-enemy-of-Israel; cross-corpus reach into Exod 17:8-16, Deut 25:17-19, Num 24:20, 1 Sam 15, 1 Sam 30, Est 3:1 (Haman the Agagite). (iii) `chorim-horites-hurrian-or-cave-dweller-identification` (vv 20-30) — the Horite-identity philological crux: Speiser-AB Hurrian-identification vs Westermann-Bartlett *chor*=cave-dweller folk-etymology; cross-corpus at Gen 14:6, Deut 2:12 + 22, 2 Chr 25:14. (iv) `yemim-hapax-hot-springs-mules-demons-lens-discipline` (v 24) — the HB-hapax *yemim* five-tradition crux (hot springs/mules/demons/warm waters/*eimim*-emendation); ASV-baseline *hot springs* preserved; lens-discipline applied (no WoH-distinctive synthesis). (v) `edom-kings-before-israel-monarchy-documentary-anchor` (v 31) — the Pentateuchal documentary-critical anchor *lifnei melokh melekh livnei Yisra'el*; Wellhausen/Eissfeldt/Friedman + Cassuto/Rashi/Ibn Ezra apparatus; the documentary-critical observation is mainstream modern scholarship and is presented as such (NOT WoH-distinctive). One extension: `pilegesh-concubine-status-class` appliesTo extended to include GEN-WOH-36:12 (Timna). The Gen 36 chapter-singular structural observations (the fourfold *hu Edom* refrain at vv 1, 8, 19, 43; the Esau-wives three-chapter-doublet on the Gen 36 side; the Bela-vs-Bil'am ben-Be'or name-doublet) are housed in the per-translation overlay per the ratified architectural-split.

**v2.33.0 adds Genesis 35 — return to Beit-El, the P-source second renaming, the death of Rachel and the birth of Binyamin, Re'uven's deed, and the death of Yitzchaq.** Ten central-glossary additions: (i) `elohei-ha-nekhar-foreign-gods-formula` (vv 2, 4) — first explicit Genesis renunciation of foreign cult-objects; cross-corpus reach at Josh 24:23-24, Judg 10:16, 1 Sam 7:3, Jer 5:19, 2 Chr 33:15; modern critical reading as evidence of household-polytheism (Halpern, M. Smith, Albertz). (ii) `chittat-elohim-divine-terror-motif` (v 5) — first Hebrew Bible *chittah*-divine-terror motif; cross-corpus at Exod 23:27, Deut 11:25, Josh 2:9, 1 Sam 14:15, 2 Chr 14:13. (iii) `nesekh-libation-drink-offering` (v 14) — only patriarchal libation in Genesis; cross-corpus reach into Mosaic sacrificial system (Exod-Lev-Num), the prophetic polemic (Hos 9:4, Jer 7:18 etc.), and the restoration register (Joel). (iv) `pilegesh-concubine-status-class` (v 22) — concubine status-class lexeme; cross-corpus at Gen 22:24, 25:6; Judg 19; 2 Sam 3:7; 1 Kgs 11:3. (v) `tzet-nephesh-departure-at-death-formula` (v 18) — explicit *tzet nephesh* death-formula; cross-corpus at Gen 25:8, 49:33, 1 Kgs 17:21-22; inverse of Gen 2:7. (vi) `goy-u-qhal-goyim-nation-company-formula` (v 11) — *nation and company of nations* patriarchal-promise formula; cross-corpus at Gen 17:5-6, 17:16, 28:3, 48:4. (vii) `reconciled-brothers-bury-father-motif` (v 29) — patriarchal-generation-arc closing motif; parallel at Gen 25:9 (Yitzchaq + Yishma'el bury Avraham). (viii) `va-yaal-elohim-divine-ascent-departure` (v 13) — divine ascent-departure formula; parallel at Gen 17:22, Judg 13:20. (ix) `nezamim-rings-cultic-amulet-association` (v 4) — ear-ring as cultic-amulet association; cross-corpus at Exod 32:2-3, Judg 8:24-26, Hos 2:15. (x) `piska-b-emtza-pasuq-masoretic-paragraph-break` (v 22) — Masoretic paragraph-break-mid-verse phenomenon, ~28 occurrences; rabbinic reception at b. Shabbat 55b, b. Sotah 13a, b. Megillah 18a. Modified entries: `ha-el-beit-el-divine-self-identification` (extends to v 7); `matzevah-sacred-pillar-patriarchal-religion` (extends to vv 14, 20); `bethel-luz-name-etymology` (extends to vv 1, 3, 6, 7, 8, 15, 16); `peru-urvu` (extends to v 11). The `el-shaddai` (v 11) and `zaqen-u-s-va-yamim` (v 29) entries' Gen 35 appliesTo were already pre-populated in earlier versions. Seven additional chapter-singular entries are housed in the per-translation overlay (v1.3.0): `yisrael-second-renaming-beit-el-p-doublet`, `binyamin-ben-oni-twelfth-tribe-aetiology`, `allon-bakhut-deborah-burial-fragment`, `migdal-eder-tower-of-flock-prophetic-reception`, `paddan-aram-binyamin-contradiction-documentary-seam`, `mamre-qiryat-arba-hevron-triple-toponym`, `reuben-bilhah-deed-succession-claim-gen-49-retrospective`.

**v2.32.0 adds Genesis 34 — Dinah at Shechem, the deceit-circumcision, the slaughter by Shim'on and Levi.** Eight central-glossary additions: (i) `innah-piel-anah-philological-crux-rape-or-shame` (v 2) — LOAD-BEARING; the chapter's foundational philological crux on the Piel of *ʿ-n-h* II; both reading-positions (rape — Sternberg, Fewell-Gunn, Frymer-Kensky, Scholz, NJPS, Alter, Sarna; status-degradation — Bechtel, Yamada, HALOT, Wenham) documented; translation preserves ASV-baseline *humbled her*; (ii) `timme-piel-defilement-language-dinah-shechem` (vv 5, 13, 27) — ritual-purity vocabulary deployed in sexual-offense context; the chapter's foundational classification-vocabulary; (iii) `nevalah-outrage-formula-communal-sexual-offense` (v 7) — the technical *outrage*-formula's first canonical occurrence; Phillips *VT* 25 (1975) reading; cross-corpus distribution at Deut 22:21, Josh 7:15, Judg 19-20, 2 Sam 13:12, Jer 29:23; (iv) `nevalah-be-yisrael-formula-collective-anachronism` (v 7) — the compound *nevalah b-Yisra'el* formula's first deployment; personal-name (Ya'aqov-as-Yisra'el) vs. collective-anachronism ambiguity; (v) `mohar-bride-price-three-verse-corpus` (v 12) — the rare lexeme's three-verse HB corpus (Gen 34:12, Exod 22:16, 1 Sam 18:25); ANE-comparative legal material (Hammurabi, Middle Assyrian Laws, Nuzi); ASV's misleading *dowry* corrected to *bride-price*; (vi) `be-mirmah-deception-ya-aqov-cycle-inheritance` (v 13) — LOAD-BEARING; the Gen 27:35 → Gen 34:13 lexical-echo; Ya'aqov's *aqav*-character pattern reaches its inheritance moment in the sons' speech; (vii) `sha-ar-ha-ir-city-gate-legal-civic-assembly` (vv 20, 24) — the HB's principal legal-civic-forum setting; cross-corpus distribution across Gen 19, Gen 23, Deut 16-25, Ruth 4, etc.; the Gen 23 / Gen 34 editorially-significant parallel; (viii) `zonah-prostitute-status-vocabulary-cross-corpus` (v 31) — the *z-n-h*-participle's widely-distributed pejorative-status-vocabulary; chapter-closing rhetorical-question; ASV's *harlot* modernized to *prostitute*. Two existing-entry expansions: (a) `kenaani-prizi-az-ba-aretz` adds GEN-WOH-34:30 to *appliesTo* — the Canaanite + Perizzite doubling-formula's character-speech-extension in Ya'aqov's rebuke; (b) `ba-yom-ha-shlishi-third-day-formula` adds GEN-WOH-34:25 to *appliesTo* — the third-day-of-circumcision-pain peak deployment of the cross-corpus formula. **v2.31.0 adds Genesis 33 — the Esav-reunion, the Sukkot-encampment, and the Shechem-altar.** Three central-glossary additions: (i) `sukkot-toponym-and-festival-etymology` (v 17) — the earliest HB occurrence of the patriarchal toponym, the lexical-family governing the Lev 23 / Deut 16 pilgrimage-festival, and the cross-corpus distribution across the Exodus-itinerary (Exod 12:37, Num 33:5-6), the Gadite tribal-allocation (Josh 13:27), Gid'on's confrontation with the men of Sukkot (Judg 8:5-16), Shlomo's bronze-foundry (1 Kgs 7:46 / 2 Chr 4:17), the *emek Sukkot* (Ps 60:8 / 108:8), and the Iron-Age site-identification at Tell Deir 'Allah; (ii) `patriarchal-land-acquisition-pattern-makhpelah-shechem` (v 19) — the foundational HB legal-title-establishment motif across Gen 23 (Avraham at Makhpelah from the Hittites), Gen 33:19 (Ya'aqov at Shechem from the Hivite-*bnei Chamor*), and Josh 24:32 (Yosef's bones buried at the Ya'aqov-parcel); with the Hivite-Hittite ethnographic-distinction (Gen 34:2's *ha-Chivvi* distinguishing the *bnei Chamor* from Gen 23's *bnei Het*); and the David-Aravnah-threshing-floor extension to 2 Sam 24:24; (iii) `el-elohei-yisrael-altar-shechem` (v 20) — the fifth and final patriarchal El-compound of the Genesis cycle, completing the pattern initiated at Gen 14 (El Elyon), Gen 16 (El Ro'i), Gen 17 (El Shaddai), Gen 21 (YHWH El Olam), and the personal-to-collective-name-transition of *Yisra'el* (Gen 33:20 personal-name → Exod 5:1 collective-name); with the Alt 1929 / Cross 1973 / Smith 2001 patriarchal-El-religion reconstruction. Two existing-entry expansions: (a) `puncta-extraordinaria-v9-elav` adds GEN-WOH-33:4 to *appliesTo* with the kiss-or-bite rabbinic-tradition (b. Sotah 13a; Bereshit Rabbah 78:9; Pirkei d-Rabbi Eliezer 37) and the cross-link to the canonical fifteen *nequdot* corpus; (b) `peniel-panim-el-panim-divine-face-encounter` adds GEN-WOH-33:10 to *appliesTo* with the Esav-reunion-as-human-counterpart-of-Penuel structural-keystone reading (Sarna, Westermann, Fokkelman, Alter) and the Speiser polite-courtly-hyperbole vs. structural-echo philological-note. The `kesitah` central entry's *appliesTo* updated to use GEN-WOH-33:19 (the WoH refId form) for the Genesis occurrence.

**v2.30.0 adds Genesis 32 — the Penuel wrestling and the Yisra'el-naming theophany; the *minchah* (gift / tribute / appeasement-offering) lexeme's two-register span (inter-political vs. cultic) and the chapter's four-occurrence Esav-tribute-strategy with its v 21 *kipper panav* cultic-vocabulary-overlap; the *Peni'el* / *Penu'el* toponym (face-of-El) with the in-text aetiology *ki-ra'iti Elohim panim el panim*, the cross-corpus *panim el panim* canonical-formula (Exod 33:11; Deut 5:4; 34:10; Judg 6:22), the *va-tinatzel nafshi* survived-theophany clause, and the cross-corpus Exod 33:20 *no man shall see me and live* tension; the name-refusal formula *lamah zeh tish'al li-shmi* with the exact Judg 13:17-18 parallel (the Manoah / Samson-annunciation episode) and the ANE magical-name background (the divine-being protects his name from invocation); and the *shalcheni ki alah ha-shachar* dawn-departure motif within the Gunkel 1901 / Frazer 1918 history-of-religions river-numen reading and the modern critical *mal'akh*-theophany-mediator reading.** Four central-glossary additions: (i) `minchah-tribute-gift-of-appeasement` (vv 14, 19, 21, 22) — the two-register lexeme and the cross-corpus distribution from Gen 4:3 (Qayin/Hevel) through the Sinaitic cult (Lev 2; Num 28-29) and the inter-political tribute-register (Judg 3:15-18; 2 Sam 8:2-6; Hos 10:6) to the eschatological *minchah* (Mal 1:11). (ii) `peniel-panim-el-panim-divine-face-encounter` (vv 31, 32) — the toponym + the cross-corpus *panim el panim* formula + the survived-theophany cluster (Gen 16:13; Judg 6:22; 13:22; Exod 33:11, 20; Isa 6:5). (iii) `name-refusal-formula` (v 30) — the exact Judg 13:18 parallel and the cross-corpus name-disclosure / name-refusal distinction with Exod 3:13-15 as the canonical-counterpoint. (iv) `dawn-constrained-divine-departure` (v 27) — the Gen 19:15 Sodom-messenger parallel and the ANE comparative-religion background (Ugaritic Shahar/Shalim; Mesopotamian night-demons; Greek chthonic *nyktis*; Egyptian Ra-night-traversal). Per-translation overlay (Genesis WoH) adds seven chapter-specific entries: `mahanayim-two-camps-divine-encounter` (vv 2-3 — the dual-form toponym and Josh 13:26 / 2 Sam 2:8 cross-corpus); `akhapprah-panav-appease-his-face` (v 21 — the *kipper panav* cultic-vocabulary import and the chapter's four-fold *panim* cluster); `yabboq-vayeavek-paronomasia` (vv 23-25 — the Yabboq / va-ye'avek / Ya'aqov three-fold word-play); `the-ish-of-penuel-anonymous-wrestler` (v 25, LOAD-BEARING — the five-tradition interpretive landscape from *mal'akh* through Christophany / Esav's-guardian-angel-Samael / river-numen-Gunkel to Jacob's-internal-other-Fokkelman, all preserved without project preemption); `kaf-yerekh-tek'a-wrestling-injury` (v 26 — the hip-socket dislocation and the rabbinic euphemism-reading); `yisrael-name-aetiology-sarah-im-elohim` (v 29, MANDATORY MAJOR — the *sarita im-Elohim* in-text aetiology, the BDB / HALOT etymology-dispute, and the five proposed etymologies); `gid-ha-nasheh-sciatic-nerve-dietary-rule` (v 33, MANDATORY MAJOR — the only narrator's-time aetiology-derived kashrut rule in the HB, the *nasheh* hapax legomenon, and the Mishnah Chullin 7 / b. Chullin 89b-103b rabbinic-halakhic-development). Cross-corpus appliesTo expansions: `malakh-elohim-messenger` (Gen 32:2); `anokhi-elohei-X-avikha-ancestral-god-formula` (Gen 32:10); `chesed-v-emet-steadfast-love-and-truth` (Gen 32:11); `patriarchal-seed-comparison-triad` (Gen 32:13 reformatted to GEN-WOH-32:13). **v2.29.0 added Genesis 31 — Ya'aqov's flight from Lavan, the stolen teraphim, and the covenant of Gal-Ed and Mitzpah; the chapter's *g-n-v* (steal) verbal cluster and the *ganav-et-lev* idiom for deception; Rachel's theft of her father's *teraphim* (the lexeme's first canonical occurrence with the Nuzi-parallel inheritance-token reading and the cross-corpus distribution across Judges, Samuel, Kings, Hosea, Zechariah, Ezekiel); the *tov-ra* merism *mi-tov ad-ra* (from good to bad) in the speech-prohibition register; the *derekh nashim* menstrual-period euphemism and Rachel's double-function ritual-purity speech-act concealing the teraphim; the *Pachad Yitzchaq* hapax divine epithet at vv 42 and 53 with the Alt 1929 / Albright 1935 / Cross 1973 / Hillers 1972-73 critical-reception dispute (the traditional *Fear* reading vs. the Albright kinship-reading); the bilingual *Yegar Sahaduta* / *Gal-Ed* witness-heap at v 47 — the only continuous Aramaic phrase in Genesis — staging the ethno-linguistic separation of the Lavan-Ya'aqov treaty; the *Mitzpah-blessing* formula *yitzef YHWH beini u-veinekha* at v 49 with the modern Christian devotional-reception inversion (the original mutual-surveillance treaty-formula vs. the affectionate-parting-blessing reception); and the deity's self-identification *anokhi ha-El Beit-El* at v 13 within the patriarchal-El-compound divine-naming pattern.** Eight new entries: (i) `ha-el-beit-el-divine-self-identification` (v 13) — the deity's self-naming with a place-name within the Alt 1929 / Cross 1973 patriarchal-El-religion reconstruction. (ii) `teraphim-household-gods` (vv 19, 30, 32, 34, 35) — the household cult-objects with the Speiser 1964 / Greenberg 1962 / van der Toorn 1990 Nuzi-parallel debate and the cross-corpus distribution. (iii) `ganav-et-lev-deceive-idiom` (vv 20, 26, 27, 39) — the fixed deception-idiom and the chapter's seven *g-n-v* deployments. (iv) `tov-ra-merism` (vv 24, 29) — the comprehensive-speech-prohibition merism with the Honeyman 1952 / Watson 1984 / Krašovec 1977 merism-criticism literature. (v) `derekh-nashim-menstrual-period-euphemism` (v 35) — the menstruation-euphemism with the Lev 15:19-30 *niddah* legislation cross-link and the chapter's ritual-purity narrative-irony. (vi) `pachad-yitzchaq-fear-of-isaac-divine-epithet` (vv 42, 53) — the hapax patriarchal divine epithet with the two-generation Alt/Albright/Cross/Hillers reception. (vii) `yegar-sahaduta-galed-bilingual-witness-heap` (vv 47, 48) — the only continuous Aramaic in Genesis with the Speiser / Westermann / Sarna authenticity-debate. (viii) `mitzpah-blessing-formula` (v 49) — the treaty-surveillance vs. devotional-benediction reception-inversion. **Editorial correction**: GEN-31:11 removed from `malakh-yhwh-messenger.appliesTo` (the verse uses *mal'akh ha-Elohim*, not *mal'akh YHWH*) and added to `malakh-elohim-messenger.appliesTo`; deferred from v2.18.0. **AppliesTo expansions**: `matzevah-sacred-pillar-patriarchal-religion` adds GEN-WOH-31:13, 45, 51, 52; `anokhi-elohei-X-avikha-ancestral-god-formula` adds GEN-WOH-31:5, 29, 42, 53; **v2.25.0 added Genesis 27 — the Yitzchaq-deathbed-blessing-deception narrative, the chapter's central irony-formula, and the patriarchal-blessing-transfer-and-irrevocability cluster.** Twelve new entries: (i) `kahah-eynayim-patriarchal-blindness-narrative-trigger` (v 1) — the *va-tikheina eynav me-r'ot* blindness-clause as the cross-corpus HB pattern of blindness-as-narrative-trigger for transferred-perception or transferred-authority (Gen 48:10 Ya'aqov; Deut 34:7 Mosheh; 1 Sam 3:2 Eli; 1 Kgs 14:4 Achiyah); the privative-*min* construction preserved. (iii) `nefesh-blessing-transfer` (vv 4, 19, 25, 31) — the fourfold-deployment of *t-varekhekha nafshi* / *t-varakhanni nafshekha* (*that my nefesh may bless you*); the *nefesh* as agent-of-blessing-speech-act; the declarative-blessing-speech-act framework per Mitchell 1987 and Wolff 1974 *Anthropology of the Old Testament*; the embodied-vital-personhood semantics preserved against post-Hellenistic *soul*-dualism. (iv) `ha-qol-qol-yaakov-deception-formula` (v 22) — the chapter's central irony-formula; the chiastic-doublet structure (article-construct-doubled across two clauses); the sense-perception-failure framework (sight blocked, touch deceived, voice registered-but-not-inferred); the rabbinic Esav-as-Rome typology (b. Bava Batra 16b, Genesis Rabbah 65:20, Lev Rab 13:5, Pirkei d'Rabbi Eliezer 38, Maharal *Netzach Yisrael*) and the Pauline reception (Rom 9:13 → Mal 1:2-3 → Gen 27) flagged-not-preempted. (v) `tal-shamayim-shemanei-aretz-blessing-formula` (v 28) — the patriarchal-fertility-blessing-formula proper; cross-corpus parallels at Deut 33:13, 33:28 (the Joseph-blessing), Hos 14:6 (*ehyeh ka-tal*), Mic 5:6, Zech 8:12; the *tirosh*-vs-*yayin* distinction; the partitive-*min* fixed by the parallel-clause; the articulated *ha-Elohim* preserved; Cross 1973 on the ancient-poetic patriarchal-blessing-tradition. (vi) `yaavdukha-amim-peoples-shall-serve-formula` (v 29) — the political-dominion-clause; the *amim* / *le'umim* peoples-and-nations fixed-pair; the Gen 25:23 prenatal-oracle parallel; the *heveh g'vir* imperative-form; the ketiv/qere on *yishtachavu* (defective-vs-full spelling, qere followed); cross-corpus formula at Gen 9:25-27 (Ham-Shem-Yefet), Num 24:9 (Bil'am), Ps 72:11 (royal-messianic), Isa 49:23 (post-exilic). (vii) `cursed-cursers-blessed-blessers-echo` (v 29) — the *orereikha arur u-m'varakheikha barukh* curse-blessing-protection-clause as the reverse-echo of Gen 12:3 (Avrahamic-call); the Avrahamic-blessing-chain-transmission (Avraham → Yitzchaq → Ya'aqov → Israel-as-a-whole at Num 24:9); distinguished from the existing `nivrakhu-bekha-niphal-hithpael` entry which covers the universal-blessing-clause; Brichto 1963 *The Problem of 'Curse' in the Hebrew Bible* on the curse-vocabulary. (viii) `va-yecherad-charadah-trembling-formula` (v 33) — the cognate-accusative-and-intensifier construction *trembled a great trembling exceedingly*; cross-corpus *charadah*-cluster at Exod 19:16 (Sinai-theophany), 1 Sam 14:15 (Philistine-rout), Dan 10:7 (angelic-vision, *charadah gedolah* near-identical); the *gam-barukh yihye* blessing-irrevocability-declaration as source-text for Heb 12:17 NT reception (*topos metanoias ouk heuren*) and rabbinic *birkhat ha-av eyn la chazarah* doctrine. (ix) `tzaaqah-gedolah-u-marah-bitter-cry` (v 34) — the second cognate-accusative of the chapter; the Esther 4:1 verbal-near-identical parallel (Mordekhai's *za'aqah gedolah u-marah*) widely-documented in modern-critical commentary (Berlin, Levenson, Sarna); cross-reference to the existing `zaaqah-outcry-of-the-oppressed` central-glossary entry; the rabbinic Esther-as-Esav-echo (Esther Rabbah, Pirkei d'Rabbi Eliezer 49) flagged-not-preempted. (x) `mi-shemanei-ha-aretz-partitive-vs-separative` (vv 39-40) — **MAJOR**: the partitive-vs-separative *mi-* ambiguity at the Esav-counter-blessing; the chapter-internal contrast with v 28 (unambiguously partitive); the Edomite-Seir-desert geographical-fit favoring separative; WoH chooses the separative reading (modern-critical default per Westermann, Speiser, Sarna-apparatus, NRSV); the partitive reading (ASV/KJV/rabbinic-harmonizing per Rashi, Ramban, Gen Rab 67:6) preserved in apparatus; the only chapter-27 verse where WoH significantly diverges from the ASV-baseline. (xi) `cherev-tichye-by-the-sword-edom-tradition` (v 40) — the sword-life-and-service clause; the *tarid* lexical-problem (the three readings — *rud*/wander, *radah*/rule, *yarad*/descend; WoH follows the dominant *rud* reading per ASV/NRSV); the *paraqta ulo me-al tzavarekha* throw-off-the-yoke idiom (Jer 30:8 verbal-near-identical); cross-corpus Edomite-tradition (2 Sam 8:14 David subdues Edom; 1 Kgs 11:14-22 Hadad's rebellion under Solomon; 2 Kgs 8:20-22 the full Edomite-throwing-off under Yehoram; 2 Kgs 16:6 permanent loss under Achaz; Mal 1:2-5 post-exilic; Obadiah; 1 Macc 5:65 Maccabean; Josephus *Ant.* 13.257-258 Hyrcanus-Idumean forced-conversion); Bartlett 1989 *Edom and the Edomites* and Knauf 1992 *ABD* 'Edom'; the rabbinic Esav-as-Rome typology noted as reception-history. **v2.24.0 adds Genesis 26 — the Yitzchaq-at-Gerar wife-sister type-scene closure, the Yitzchaq-Avimelekh covenant doublet of Gen 21, and the well-naming triad.** Six new entries: (i) `mishmeret-mitzvot-chuqim-torot-fourfold-covenant` (v 5) — the fourfold Sinaitic legal-stipulation cluster anachronistically applied to Avraham, the chapter's clearest documentary-source signal (Wellhausen 1885; von Rad 1956; Westermann 1985; Sarna 1989; Friedman 1987, 2003; Levinson 1997); the rabbinic-traditional Avraham-observed-Torah-by-anticipation reading (m. Qidd. 4:14, b. Yoma 28b, Gen Rab 64:4, Rashi) is flagged-not-preempted. (ii) `metzacheq-conjugal-vs-contested-disambiguation` (v 8) — the piel-participle *m-tzacheq* in the conjugal-tenderness register at the Yitzchaq-Rivkah window-scene, disambiguated from the contested Gen 21:9 Yishma'el usage and from the wider HB *tz-ch-q* polysemy (Gen 17:17, 18:12, 19:14, 21:6, 21:9, 26:8, 39:14-17; Exod 32:6; Judg 16:25; 2 Sam 2:14); the wordplay with Yitzchaq's own name preserved. (iii) `esek-sitnah-rechovot-well-name-triad` (vv 20-22) — the three-well place-name etymology sequence; the *s-t-n* root-connection of *Sitnah* to *ha-satan* of Job 1-2 and Zech 3 is flagged-but-not-conflated (the place-name and the adversarial-figure share the root but inhabit distinct semantic registers). The structural progression — contention → opposition → room — encodes Yitzchaq's pastoral-pacifist character. (iv) `al-tira-fear-not-divine-encouragement-formula` (v 24) — one of the HB's most distributionally extensive divine-speech-openers, recurring across the Torah, Historical Books, Prophets, and post-exilic literature with particularly dense Isaian and Jeremianic-prophetic-call deployment; the ANE context (Mari-letters, Neo-Assyrian prophecy-collections) documented in Conrad 1985 *Fear Not Warrior* and Nissinen 2003 *Prophets and Prophecy in the Ancient Near East*. (v) `anokhi-elohei-X-avikha-ancestral-god-formula` (v 24) — the *Anokhi Elohei Avraham avikha* ancestral-god self-disclosure formula, foundational for Albrecht Alt's 1929 *Der Gott der Väter* reconstruction of the patriarchal-period ancestral-god religion; the Exod 3:6 burning-bush triadic-patriarchal compound is the keystone deployment. The Alt reconstruction has been refined by Cross 1973 (Canaanite-El-cult-identification) and partly critiqued by Van Seters 1975 and Thompson 1974; the modern critical consensus (Miller 2000, Smith 1990/2002) holds the broad framework plausible while the specific 2nd-millennium-BCE historicity remains contested. (vi) `morat-ruach-bitterness-of-spirit-esau-hittite-wives` (vv 34-35) — the chapter-closing narrative-pivot toward Gen 27, with the Esav-Hittite-wife-list inconsistency across Gen 26:34, 28:9, 36:2-3 flagged as a documentary-source-critical problem. AppliesTo expansions: `be-er-sheva-folk-etymology` → GEN-WOH-26:23, 26:33; `elohim-imkha-divine-presence-formula` → GEN-WOH-26:28 (the Yitzchaq-Avimelekh doublet of the Gen 21:22 formula); `wife-sister-type-scene` already covers Gen 26:1-11; `va-yera-yhwh-theophany` already covers Gen 26:2, 26:24; `qara-b-shem-yhwh` already covers Gen 26:25. The chapter's editorial density — Yitzchaq, the least-narratively-developed of the three patriarchs, receives the most-densely-formulaic divine-encouragement and the most-elaborated wife-sister-Avimelekh-Be'er-Sheva doublet of the Avraham-cycle — is the chapter's structural signature. // **v2.6.0 reached Job 38. v2.7.0 added Job 40-41 (9 entries) — the Behemoth and Leviathan passages, the canonical Hebrew Bible dragon-tradition material. Leviathan is identified with the *tannin* of Isaiah 27:1 (linking to the taninim entry from Gen 1:21, glossary v2.0.0) and with the Ugaritic *Lotan*. v2.8.0 adds Job 42 (7 entries), closing the book of Job. The new entries cover the book's keystone closing material: the ear-vs-eye direct-experiential epistemology of v 5 (shema-ozen-eyni-ra-atkha), the three-way philological dispute on v 6 (em-as-v-nikhamti-al-afar-va-efer), the YHWH-verdict-on-the-friends inversion of vv 7-8 (lo-dibartem-elay-nekhonah), the pan-HB restoration phrase shav et-shvut (v 10) — a cross-corpus entry that will govern future translations across Deuteronomy, the Major and Minor Prophets, the Psalms, and Lamentations — the archaic currency-unit kesitah (v 11; also Gen 33:19 and Josh 24:32), the three sense-bearing daughter-names of v 14 (yemimah-ketziah-keren-happukh), and the patriarchal-good-death formula of v 17 (zaqen-u-s-va-yamim; cross-corpus: Abraham, Isaac, David, Jehoiada, Job). One translation change is driven by glossary in this pass: shav et-shvut moves the English from ASV's 'turned the captivity' to the modern-scholarly-consensus 'restored the fortunes' (Bracke 1985; Dahood; NRSV/NJPS/Newsom). The book of Job is wisdom-literature, not divine-council-disclosure; the WoH lens stays quiet in this chapter, surfacing only at vv 7-8 to note the verdict-inversion's connection back to the bnei-elohim framing of chs 1-2. // **v2.9.0 opens Genesis 12 — the start of the patriarchal narratives.** Thirteen new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary: the doubled-imperative *lekh-lekha* of v 1 (recurring only at Gen 22:2, forming the Abraham-cycle inclusio); the niphal/hithpael blessing-form dispute at v 3 (the load-bearing universal-promise verse, with the Pauline-reception complication of Gal 3:8 vs. the modern critical middle/reflexive reading of Sarna/Speiser/NJPS); the *nefesh asher asu* of v 5 (proselytes-made vs. slaves-acquired, the classical rabbinic vs. modern critical split); the sacred-tree-oracle site *elon Moreh* of v 6 (with the patriarchal sacred-tree pattern across Shechem, Bethel, Ophrah, etc.); the first patriarchal theophany-formula *va-yera YHWH el-Avram* of v 7 (recurring across the Abraham-Isaac-Jacob arc); the seed-land covenant-nucleus *l-zarakha eten et-ha-aretz* of v 7 (expanded across 13:14-17, 15:7-21, 17:8, etc.); the cult-site invocation *qara b-shem YHWH* of v 8 (resuming the Seth-Enosh tradition of Gen 4:26); the wife-sister type-scene framing of vv 10-20 (Alter's canonical 1981 treatment, three instances at Gen 12 / 20 / 26); the beautiful-woman formula *eshet yefat mar'eh* of v 11 (a Hebrew Bible stock-phrase across Rebekah, Rachel, Joseph, Abigail, Tamar, Abishag, Esther); the say-sister strategy *imri-na achoti at* of v 13 (the ethically-debated patriarchal lapse); the camel anachronism of v 16 (Sapir-Hen and Ben-Yosef 2013 archaeological consensus on Iron Age Levantine camel domestication); the proto-Exodus plague-formula *naga / nega'im* of v 17 (verbal link forward to Exod 11:1); and the Abram-Pharaoh expulsion verb *va-y-shalchu* of v 20 (verbally identical to Exod 12:33). The chapter is not high-lens-leverage — the WoH posture surfaces only at the theophany verses (7, 17) to note continuity with the divine-descent pattern of chs 1-11. // **v2.10.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 13** — the return from Mitsrayim and the separation from Lot. Six new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary: the Canaanite-Perizzite redactional parenthetical of v 7 (paired with the simpler 12:6 form, the chapter's most-cited Documentary Hypothesis flag); the *gan YHWH* Eden back-reference at v 10 (a load-bearing inter-textual move framing the Sodom plain as a second Eden and its destruction in ch 19 as a second Eden-loss type-scene; Westermann, Sarna, Hamilton, Alter consensus); the *sa eynekha* lift-your-eyes formula introduced at v 14 (recurring across 10+ patriarchal verses as the corpus's standard seeing / visionary-perception formula); the patriarchal seed-comparison triad of dust (13:16) + stars (15:5) + sand (22:17), tracked as a deliberate cosmographic distribution across earth, sky, and shore; the covenantal *hithallech ba-aretz* land-walking formula of v 17 (Daube 1947 *Studies in Biblical Law*, with parallels at Josh 1:3, Josh 18:8, Deut 11:24); and the *elonei Mamre* sacred-tree site of v 18 (parallel to but distinct from *elon Moreh* of 12:6 — the recurring locus of Abram's residence, the three-visitors theophany of ch 18, and the Machpelah burial-narratives of chs 23, 25, 49, 50). The chapter's only high-lens-leverage moment is the *gan YHWH* back-reference at v 10; the rest is patriarchal-narrative scaffolding. // **v2.11.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 14** — the war of the four kings against the five, Lot's rescue, the Melchizedek-and-El-Elyon encounter, and Abram's refusal of Sodom's spoils. Ten new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary, concentrated in the Melchizedek block (vv 18-22). The cross-corpus, scholarly-load-bearing material: the *Refa'im / Zuzim / Emim / Anakim / Nephilim* giant-peoples chain of v 5 (with the explicit Deut 2:10-12, 2:20-21, 3:11 equivalences and the lens-relevant link back to the Gen 6:1-4 Nephilim — claim_type=inferred); the *ha-Ivri* gentilic-etymology dispute of v 13 (the Eber-eponymous reading, the habiru/apiru social-class reading from the Amarna corpus, and the *eber* Trans-Euphrates reading — claim_type=direct, the dispute itself is direct); the *chanikhav* hapax of v 14 (the unique-occurrence noun from ch-n-k, the Egyptian *ḥnk* execration-text parallel, the 318=Eliezer gematria from Gen Rab 43:2 — claim_type=inferred). The Melchizedek block (vv 18-22) — the highest-lens-leverage block in the entire Abraham cycle to date — contributes six entries: *melchizedek* (the theophoric vs. honorific name-readings, the *Tzedek* Jebusite-deity question, the Ps 110:4 cross-reference, the 11QMelchizedek and Heb 5-7 reception-traditions flagged-not-preempted — claim_type=direct on the name itself); *melekh-shalem-jerusalem* (the canonical Jerusalem identification from Ps 76:3 and 1QapGen, with the independent-site and divine-epithet alternatives — claim_type=direct); *kohen-first-melchizedek* (the first-occurrence-of-*kohen* status, the pre-Aaronic pre-Sinai priesthood, the *kohen l-El-Elyon* not-l-YHWH collocation — claim_type=direct); *el-elyon* (the divine name; the West-Semitic *El* + *Elyon* compound and the Phoenician *Elioun* of Sanchuniathon/Philo of Byblos; the Deut 32:8-9 divine-council datum with the 4QDeut^j / LXX *bnei Elohim* reading flagged; the Num 24:16 Balaam occurrence and the wider Psalter distribution — claim_type=direct on the name, the West-Semitic-pantheon framework is scholarly synthesis but mainstream); *koneh-shamayim-va-aretz* (the q-n-h root duality of acquire-vs-create, the Ugaritic *qny ʾrṣ* and Phoenician Karatepe *El qoneh ʾerets* and Hittite *Elkunirsa* parallels, the inherited West-Semitic high-god title — claim_type=direct on the lexical-philological facts); *ma'aser-first-pre-sinai* (the first occurrence of the tithe in the Hebrew Bible, the pre-Sinai pre-Aaronic locus, the Heb 7:2-10 Levi-through-Abraham reception flagged-not-preempted, the cross-reference to Jacob's Bethel tithe at Gen 28:22 — claim_type=direct). The chapter's keystone theological move is captured by *yhwh-el-elyon-apposition* at v 22: the Genesis editor's explicit appositional identification of YHWH with the El Elyon of the Melchizedek tradition — the canonical syncretism (claim_type=direct on the apposition itself; the harmonization-reading from Cross 1973 and Smith 2001 is scholarly synthesis but mainstream). The lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (matching ASV at every divergence point), while the glossary apparatus and per-verse commentary carry the lens-relevant material — the divine-council reading of Deut 32:8-9, the West-Semitic-pantheon context of *El* and *Elyon*, the pre-Israelite priesthood and the giant-peoples chain. The Tzeboim ketiv/qere at vv 2 and 8 is resolved to the qere ('Tzeboim') and noted in commentary without a separate glossary entry (Editor's discretion: orthographic, not lexical). // **v2.12.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 15** — the covenant between the pieces (*brit bein ha-betarim*) and the foundational covenant-of-land promise. Ten new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary. The cross-corpus prophetic-revelation cluster at v 1 contributes three entries: *davar-yhwh-prophetic-formula* (the inaugural occurrence of *va-y'hi devar-YHWH*, the standard prophetic-introduction formula across Samuel/Kings/Isaiah/Jeremiah/Ezekiel/the Twelve, 250+ occurrences); *machazeh-prophetic-vision* (the prophetic-vision lexeme distinct from *chazon* and *mar'eh*; sparse recurrence at Num 24:4, 16 and Ezek 13:7); *magen-shield-of-abram* (the *anokhi magen lakh* divine self-introduction, the *magen*-of-the-fathers epithet recurring at Deut 33:29, 2 Sam 22:3, 22:31, Ps 3:4, 18:3, 28:7, 33:20, 84:12, 115:9-11, 144:2, Prov 30:5). The compound divine-name *adonai-yhwh-divine-name* at vv 2, 8 is the first patriarchal occurrence of the masoretic *qere perpetuum* substitution (*YHWH* with Elohim-vowels where *Adonai* already precedes), becoming a fixed Ezekielian formula. The highest-reception-weight verse in the chapter is *aman-tzedakah-counted-as-righteousness* at v 6 — the Hebrew lexical core (*he'emin* hiphil of *aman*; *yachshebeha lo tzedakah*; the ambiguous feminine antecedent) is direct, with the downstream Pauline reception (Rom 4:3, 4:9, 4:22; Gal 3:6; James 2:23) and the Reformation justification-by-faith superstructure flagged as downstream-Greek-and-Latin reception and NOT preempted by the translation. The covenant-cutting ritual block contributes the most lens-load-bearing entries: *brit-bein-ha-betarim* (vv 9-11, 17; the ANE parallels at Mari ARM II 37/II 80, Alalakh AT 1/76, the Sefire treaties KAI 222-224, Jer 34:18-20; Weinfeld 1970 *JAOS* 90, Mendenhall 1955, Hillers 1969, McCarthy 1978); *tardemah-divine-induced-deep-sleep* at v 12 (back-reference to Gen 2:21 Adam; forward to 1 Sam 26:12, Job 4:13, Job 33:15, Isa 29:10; LXX *ekstasis*); the *tanur-ashan-lappid-esh-theophany* at v 17 (the chapter's keystone fire-and-smoke theophany; cross-references to Exod 19:18 Sinai, Exod 13:21 pillar-of-fire, Isa 6:4, Ezek 1; the unilateral-passage-while-Abram-sleeps establishing the Yahweh-only covenant). The chronology block at vv 13-16 contributes *400-years-affliction-prophecy* (the 400 / 430 chronology dispute across Gen 15:13, Exod 12:40, Acts 7:6, Gal 3:17; the LXX/Samaritan harmonization; the four-generation-vs-400-years tension at v 16) and *avon-ha-emori-iniquity-not-yet-complete* at v 16 (the *lo shalem avon ha-Emori* cup-of-iniquity formula, Deut 9:4-5 / Lev 18:24-28 / Lev 20:23 cross-references, the 1 Thess 2:16 Pauline downstream reception). The covenant-formula and land-promise at v 18 contributes *karat-brit-cut-covenant* (the standard Hebrew covenant-cutting idiom, ~80 cross-corpus occurrences) and *ten-nations-of-canaan* (the unique ten-fold enumeration at vv 19-21 — the Qeini/Qenizi/Qadmoni triad unique to this verse, with the maximal Euphrates land-claim of v 18 as the geographical pairing). The chapter is high-lens-leverage but the lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (matching ASV at every divergence point), while the glossary apparatus and per-verse commentary carry the lens-relevant material — the unilateral-divine-passage at v 17 with Abram in *tardemah*, the cross-corpus fire-theophany corpus, the *tardemah* divine-revelation chain back to Gen 2:21, and the ANE covenant-cutting parallels. The Pauline reception of v 6 and of v 16 is flagged-but-not-preempted at every occurrence. // **v2.13.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 16** — Hagar and Ishmael, and the first occurrence of *ma'lakh YHWH* (messenger of YHWH) in the Hebrew Bible. Eight new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary. The chapter's highest-lens-leverage entry is *malakh-yhwh-messenger* (claim_type=direct): the inaugural occurrence of the messenger-of-YHWH figure, with the three modern critical positions on messenger-identity presented in parallel (Heidt 1949's distinct-envoy reading, Westermann 1985 / von Rad 1972's identity reading, Hamori 2008 / Sommer 2009 / MacDonald 2009's literary-theological-device reading) and none preempted; the WoH translation choice *messenger of YHWH* (preserving the Hebrew etymology over the conventional *angel*) is editorially neutral on the identity question. The cross-corpus distribution of the figure is documented at 60+ verses across Genesis, Exodus, Numbers, Joshua, Judges, Samuel-Kings, Isaiah, Zechariah, Psalms. The post-exilic developed angelology is flagged as a distinct stratum. The chapter's ANE-comparative entry is *shifchah-substitute-wife-arrangement* (claim_type=direct): the substitute-wife legal institution attested at Code of Hammurabi §146 and Nuzi HSS V 67 (Speiser 1964 reading the Nuzi parallels as closer in detail than the Hammurabi); the lexeme governs both the Gen 16 Hagar narrative and the Gen 30 Bilhah/Zilpah narratives. The forensic-speech-act entry *chamas-forensic-curse-formula* (claim_type=direct) covers Sarai's v 5 declaration *chamasi alekha* paired with *yishpot YHWH beyni u-veynekha* — the *chamas* register and the YHWH-litigation idiom together constituting a formal legal speech-act, with cross-corpus distribution across the prophetic-indictment corpus. The Hebrew-grammatical entry *harbah-arbeh-doubled-infinitive-intensive* (claim_type=direct) covers the doubled-infinitive-absolute intensifying construction at v 10 and its corpus-wide distribution (Gen 2:16-17, 3:16, 17:2, 17:20, 22:17, etc.); the *rbh* doubled-infinitive subset is concentrated in the patriarchal seed-multiplication promises, with Gen 16:10 the only such promise addressed to a woman and a non-Israelite. The name-etymology entry *yishmael-name-etymology* (claim_type=direct) covers the first in-text patriarchal name-etymology in the Hebrew Bible and its place in the Yishma'el / Shim'on / Re'uven shared-verb cluster; the Knauf 1989 *Ismael* North-Arabian-tribal historical reconstruction is included as direct documentary background. The ethnographic-and-reception entry *pere-adam-wild-ass-prophecy* (claim_type=direct) is the chapter's most carefully framed entry: the wild-ass-of-a-man hapax compound is read on its Hebrew-philological terms (the *pere*-pastoralist register supported by Job 39:5-8 and the wider corpus), with the modern critical consensus (Westermann/Sarna/Wenham/Hamilton on the wilderness-pastoralist mode-of-life reading) explicitly endorsed and the Islamic reception (Sura 14:39, 19:54-55; the early-Islamic tafsir tradition's positive desert-freedom reading; Gen Rab on Ishmael) flagged but not preempted, AND the 19th-20th century Orientalist racializing receptions explicitly named for editorial caution and rejected as not supported by the Hebrew (Said 1978). The textual-variant entry *el-roi-and-halom-elohim-variant* (claim_type=direct) covers the dual crux of v 13: the divine name *El Ro'i* (the first divine-name-naming by a woman in the Hebrew Bible, and by a non-Israelite Egyptian woman at that; Trible 1984, Reis 2002) and the MT *halom* / LXX-emended *Elohim* textual variant, with the seeing-God-and-living anxiety parallels at Exod 33:20, Judg 6:22-23, Judg 13:22 documented; the WoH choice preserves the MT *halom* over the *Elohim* emendation, consistent with the established WoH textual-conservative practice. The well-naming entry *beer-lachai-roi* (claim_type=direct) covers the patriarchal well-naming pattern (Be'er-Sheva, Eseq/Sitnah/Rechovot, Marah, etc.) and the cross-corpus recurrence of the Be'er Lachai Ro'i well at Gen 24:62 and 25:11 (Isaac settling at the Hagar-encounter site). The chapter is mid-to-high lens-leverage but the lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (matching ASV at every divergence point, including the *halom* preservation and the geographical-ambiguity preservation of *al-pnei kol echav yishkon* at v 12), while the glossary apparatus and per-verse commentary carry the lens-relevant material — the modern critical messenger-identity scholarship, the ANE substitute-wife legal background, the Knauf 1989 Ishmaelite-confederation historical reconstruction, the seeing-God-and-living anxiety formulae corpus, and the foreign-woman-cultic-site register. The chapter's *malakh-yhwh-messenger* entry is the highest-lens-leverage call of the chapter and is held to strict accuracy-above-lens discipline: the three modern critical positions are presented in parallel and none is preempted; project-specific synthesis is reserved for the wiki and long-form material downstream. // **v2.14.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 17** — the *El Shaddai* self-introduction, the *brit milah* (covenant of circumcision), the Avram → Avraham and Sarai → Sarah name-changes, and the Yitzchaq promise. Nine new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary, and three existing entries are expanded in *appliesTo* to cover the Gen 17 occurrences (additive-only, no semver-major modifications). The chapter's two highest-lens-leverage entries are *el-shaddai* and *brit-milah-circumcision-covenant*, both held to strict accuracy-above-lens discipline. The *el-shaddai* entry presents the first patriarchal occurrence of the name (recurring at Gen 28:3, 35:11, 43:14, 48:3, 49:25) with three modern critical etymologies in parallel — the traditional *shadad* / *destroy-be-powerful* reading behind LXX *pantokrator* and Vulgate *omnipotens*; the Akkadian *šadû* / *mountain* reading of Cross 1973 and Albright 1935; the West-Semitic divine-epithet *Šaddai* reading of Ouellette 1969 and Hess 2007, with the Deir ʿAlla *shdyn* inscription as direct documentary support. The *karet-penalty-cut-off-from-people* entry covers the standard P/H covenant-violation sanction across ~30 Pentateuchal occurrences with the four positions on the penalty's nature (Milgrom 1991 divine-direct, Phillips 1970 excommunication, Tigay JPS Deut 1996 premature death, Levine JPS Lev 1989 extinction of line) and the Wold 1979 monograph as standard reference. The three existing entries expanded in *appliesTo*: *brit-olam* (from GEN-9:16 only, expanded to include the patriarchal occurrences at GEN-17:7, 17:13, 17:19); *meqim-brit* (from GEN-9:9 and GEN-9:11, expanded to GEN-17:7, 17:19, 17:21); *yishmael-name-etymology* (existing list expanded to include the Gen 17 occurrences at 17:18, 17:20, 17:23, 17:25, 17:26). The chapter is mid-to-high lens-leverage but the lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (matching ASV at every divergence point, including *El Shaddai* preserved over *God Almighty*, *tamim* rendered *blameless* rather than *perfect*, *nefesh* preserved per chapter-2 convention, and the patriarchal name-transliterations *Avraham* / *Sarah* / *Yitzchaq* / *Yishma'el* preserved). The apparatus carries the lens-relevant material — the West-Semitic patriarchal-El cult inheritance behind *El Shaddai*, the P-document harmonization claim at Exod 6:3, the ANE comparative circumcision evidence, the Knauf 1989 North-Arabian Ishmaelite-confederation reconstruction. The translation does not take a Jewish-vs-Christian supersessionist position on circumcision; the entry treats the institution in its Hebrew-Bible register and flags the downstream receptions as downstream. // **v2.15.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 18** — the three visitors at the oaks of Mamre and Avraham's intercession for Sodom, the highest-lens-leverage theophany in the Abraham cycle. Eleven new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary. The chapter's defining feature is the singular-plural divine-presentation oscillation that the *shloshah-anashim-three-visitors-theophany* entry documents at length: YHWH (sg) appears at v 1 → three men (pl) appear at v 2 → Avraham addresses *adonai* (sg) at v 3 → plural imperatives from v 4 → *they ate* (pl) at v 8 → *he said* (sg) at v 10 → YHWH named again at v 13 → *the men* (pl) rise at v 16 → at v 22 the figures split, with the men going on to Sodom (the two who arrive there as *shnei ha-malakhim* at Gen 19:1; cross-reference to *malakh-yhwh-messenger*) while Avraham stands before YHWH (sg). The entry presents the three modern critical positions on the singular-plural oscillation in parallel (Westermann's J-document theophany, Sarna's literary-effect, Letellier's chiastic Mamre/Sodom diptych) and the two anthropomorphic-realism / fluidity readings (Hamori 2008, Sommer 2009), and flags the Christian-tradition reception (Heb 13:2 *hospitality of strangers*; Augustine *De Trinitate* II.10-11; the patristic and Eastern-Orthodox *Trinity of Mamre* tradition culminating in Andrei Rublev's icon of 1411) as downstream-not-preempted. The *tzedakah-u-mishpat-justice-pair* entry covers the first occurrence of the foundational HB justice-formula at v 19, with the prophetic-and-Psalter cross-corpus distribution (Amos 5:24; Isa 1:21, 5:7, 28:17; Jer 22:3, 22:15, 23:5; Ezek 18, 33, 45; Ps 33, 72, 89, 97, 99, 103; Mic 6:8 with *chesed*; Prov 1, 2, 8, 21; Weinfeld 1995 hendiadys-reading). The *zaaqah-outcry-of-the-oppressed* entry covers the forensic-cry lexeme at vv 20-21, with the load-bearing cross-corpus distribution (Gen 4:10 Abel's blood; Exod 2:23-25, 3:7-9 Israel in Egypt; Exod 22:22-24 widow-and-orphan; 1 Sam 9:16; James 5:4) and the *outcry against Sodom* (cry of the oppressed) consensus reading corroborated by Ezek 16:49. The *divine-descent-to-investigate-pattern* entry covers the *erdah-na v-er'eh* formula at v 21 as a recurring divine-descent pattern across the primeval and patriarchal narratives (Gen 11:5, 11:7 Babel; Exod 3:8 burning bush; Exod 19:11, 18, 20 Sinai; Mic 1:3; Isa 64:1); the lens-relevance is significant but the entry stays in mainstream scholarly register. The *tiqun-soferim-yhwh-stood-before-abraham* entry covers the canonical scribal-emendation tradition at v 22 (the original *YHWH still stood before Avraham* preserved in the Masoretic apparatus, emended to *Avraham still stood before YHWH* for reverence), with the standard list of eighteen *tiqune soferim*, the canonical McCarthy 1981 monograph, the skeptical Tov 2012 / Würthwein 1995 textual-critical position, and the rabbinic-source citations (b. Megillah 25b; Tanchuma; Bereshit Rabbah 49:7; Mekhilta Shirta 6). The *abraham-intercession-bargaining* entry covers the 50→45→40→30→20→10 sequence at vv 22-33, the foundational HB intercession-pericope, with the *ha-shofet kol ha-aretz* universal-judge formula at v 25 (Brueggemann 1997 on the *anti-divine-injustice* prophetic register) and the cross-corpus intercession-tradition (Exod 32:11-14 Moses for Israel; Num 14:13-20; 1 Sam 12:23; Job 42:8-10; Amos 7:1-9; Ezek 9:8, 11:13). The *afar-va-efer-creatural-humility* entry covers the *dust and ashes* formula at v 27, the rare compound (only three HB occurrences: Gen 18:27, Job 30:19, Job 42:6), with cross-reference to the existing *em-as-v-nikhamti-al-afar-va-efer* Job entry and the Second-Temple deuterocanonical parallels (Sir 10:9; 17:32; 40:3). The *puncta-extraordinaria-v9-elav* entry covers the supralinear-dots Masoretic notation at v 9 (one of the canonical fifteen *puncta extraordinaria*), with the two readings in parallel (the traditional rabbinic textual-reservation reading from Soferim 6:3 / Bereshit Rabbah 48:15 / b. Bava Metzia 87a; the modern text-critical editorial-flag reading from Tov 2012, McCarthy 1981, Yeivin 1980). The chapter is the highest-lens-leverage chapter in the Abraham cycle to date, but the lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (matching ASV-baseline at every divergence point, including the unhedged *and they ate*, the literal singular-plural oscillation preservation, and the MT *Avraham still stood before YHWH*); the glossary apparatus carries the lens-relevant material — the modern critical theophany scholarship, the anthropomorphic-realism and fluidity readings, the recurring divine-descent pattern, the cross-corpus justice-and-outcry formulae, and the Masoretic-emendation tradition. The Christian-Trinitarian and hospitality-of-strangers receptions are flagged but not preempted. // **v2.16.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 19** — the destruction of Sodom and Amorah, Lot's rescue, the daughters' incest, and the Moab/Ammon etiology. The most physically-described divine destruction event in the Hebrew Bible. Eleven new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary, and four existing entries are expanded in *appliesTo* to cover the Gen 19 occurrences (additive-only, no semver-major modifications): *malakh-yhwh-messenger* (+GEN-19:1, GEN-19:15 — the *shnei ha-malakhim* who are the two of Gen 18's three figures continuing on to Sodom), *shloshah-anashim-three-visitors-theophany* (+GEN-19:1 — the arithmetic resolution of the Gen 18 split), *divine-eating-anthropomorphism* (already covered GEN-19:3, no change needed), *yitzchaq-name-etymology-laughter-pattern* (+GEN-19:14 — the piel-mockery *m'tzacheq* in the wider patriarchal *tzachaq* cluster), and *vayyizkor-elohim* (+GEN-19:29, GEN-30:22, EXOD-2:24 — the broader *zakhar*-with-divine-subject cluster). The chapter's eleven new entries: *sodom-as-sodomy-reception-vs-inhospitality* (the reception-history flashpoint of the entire chapter, presenting the patristic / medieval *sodomy* reading and the modern critical *gang-rape-violence and inhospitality* reading in parallel and preempting neither; Carden 2004, Tornkvist 1998, Letellier 1995, Loader 1990; with Ezek 16:48-50 decisive evidence for the inhospitality reading and Judg 19 the canonical parallel-scene); *lot-offers-daughters-modern-ethical-reading* (the modern feminist-critical reception of Lot's daughter-offer at v 8; Trible 1984 *Texts of Terror*, Fewell and Gunn 1993, Bach 1997/1999, Exum 1996, with the ancient ANE hospitality-and-honor reading also documented); *sanverim-blindness-hapax* (the hapax *sanverim* of v 11, recurring only at 2 Kgs 6:18 — Elisha and the Aramean army; the possible Akkadian *šunwurum* dazzling-blindness etymology); *chemlat-yhwh-compassion-formula* (the lexeme *chemlah* at v 16 distinct from *chesed* and *rachamim*; cross-corpus 2 Sam 12:4, Mal 3:17, Joel 2:18, Jon 4:10-11, Ezek 9:5); *al-tabit-acharekha-do-not-look-back* (the do-not-look-back prohibition at v 17, with the Luke 17:32 NT recall *remember Lot's wife* and the comparative ANE / Greek pattern at Orpheus, Inanna's descent, and Adapa — flagged as comparative mythological observation not as direct dependence); *gophrit-va-esh-destruction-formula* (the *sulfur and fire* canonical HB destruction-formula at v 24 — one of the most cross-corpus-reachable entries in the glossary: Deut 29:22, Job 18:15, Isa 30:33, 34:9, Ezek 38:22, Ps 11:6; NT recall at Luke 17:29 and the four Revelation *lake of fire and sulfur* verses 14:10, 19:20, 20:10, 21:8); *me-et-yhwh-min-ha-shamayim-two-powers-debate* (the doubled *YHWH* in v 24 — the foundational text of the rabbinic two-powers debate at b. Sanhedrin 38b, and of Justin Martyr's binitarian Logos-exegesis at *Dialogue with Trypho* 56-59; modern scholarly Segal 1977, Boyarin 2004 and 2012; high lens-leverage entry held in strict accuracy-above-lens discipline, the lens stays in the wiki); *mahapekhah-overthrowing-formula* (the *hafakh / mahapekhah* overthrow-verb at v 25 — the standard HB designation for the Sodom event; cross-corpus chain Deut 29:22, Isa 1:7 and 13:19, Jer 49:18 and 50:40, Amos 4:11, Lam 4:6); *netziv-melach-pillar-of-salt* (the *pillar of salt* etiology at v 26 — the Mt. Sodom / Jabal Usdum halite formations, Josephus *Ant.* I.11.4, the Bab edh-Dhra Bronze-Age destruction archaeology of Rast and Schaub 1974 / 2003); *qitor-ha-kivshan-furnace-smoke-comparison* (the furnace-smoke comparison at v 28 — one of the most physically concrete destruction-images in the HB, with the cross-corpus chain to Exod 9:8 and the editorially significant verbatim echo at Exod 19:18 *as the smoke of the furnace* of the Sinai theophany; the Tall el-Hammam Late-Bronze-Age cosmic-airburst hypothesis of Bunch et al. 2021 cited as scientific hypothesis, not as project-distinctive synthesis; high lens-leverage entry held in mainstream scientific register); *moab-ammon-etiology-incest* (the folk-etymologies of Moab from *me-av* and Ben-Ammi from *ben ammi* at vv 37-38 — the canonical HB polemic-against-neighbor-peoples; Deut 23:4-5 exclusion from the assembly, the foundational tension of the book of Ruth's Davidic genealogy, and 1 Kgs 11 / 2 Kgs 3 / Neh 13:1-3 reception; Mesha Stele evidence the etymology is polemical-etiological not philological-historical; Bauer 1999, Sarna, Westermann). The chapter is the most physically-described divine destruction event in the HB and is high lens-leverage at two verses (v 24 *me-et YHWH min ha-shamayim*; v 28 *qitor ha-kivshan*), but the lens stays strictly in the apparatus — the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (ASV-baseline at every divergence point), and the two highest-leverage entries (*me-et-yhwh-min-ha-shamayim-two-powers-debate*, *qitor-ha-kivshan-furnace-smoke-comparison*) are held in mainstream rabbinic / patristic / modern critical / scientific register. Project-distinctive WoH synthesis (the Tall el-Hammam impact hypothesis as evidence for technological-civilization destruction; the two-powers debate as evidence for the Elohim-plurality reading) is reserved for the wiki and long-form material downstream. The reception-history of the sodom-as-sodomy reading and the modern feminist-critical reading of Lot's daughter-offer are presented in parallel without preempting either. // **v2.17.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 20** — the second wife-sister type-scene, Avraham at Gerar with Avimelekh. Largely a glossary-recycling chapter (the [wife-sister-type-scene](#wife-sister-type-scene), [imri-na-achoti-at](#imri-na-achoti-at), [elohim-as-translation](#elohim-as-translation), and [eretz](#eretz) entries drive most of the chapter's lexical decisions), but it contains **two foundational firsts** that demand dedicated entries: the **first occurrence in the Hebrew Bible of *navi* (prophet)** at v 7, applied to Avraham and defining the prophetic role as intercession (the *navi-first-occurrence-abraham* entry, with cross-corpus distribution to Exod 32, Num 11, 14, 1 Sam 7, 12, Jer 7, 11, 14, Amos 7); and the **first occurrence of *hithpallel* (to pray, intercede)** at v 17, performing the function v 7 defined (the *hithpallel-first-prayer-occurrence* entry, forward-loaded to Num 11/21, 1 Sam 1/7/8/12, 1 Kgs 8, 2 Kgs 19/20, Jer 7/11/14, Dan 9, Neh 1/2; Greenberg 1983, Miller 1994, Balentine 1993). The two firsts coincide structurally at the single chapter — the prophetic role and the prophetic function are named together. Five further entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary: *divine-dream-communication-formula* (v 3 *ba-chalom ha-lailah*, the first night-dream-revelation formula in the HB, cross-corpus to Gen 28/31/37/40-41 and 1 Kgs 3:5; Husser 1999, Lichtenstein 1968 JANES 1); *b-tom-levav-u-v-niqyon-kappai-moral-innocence* (v 5, the doubled-noun forensic-moral-innocence idiom; cross-corpus to Ps 24:4, 26:6, 73:13, Job 22:30); *yirat-elohim-moral-restraint-motif* (v 11, the fear-of-the-Elohim chain across Gen 22:12 [Aqedah], Gen 42:18 [Joseph], Exod 1:17 [midwives], Exod 20:20 [Sinai], Job 1:1, Prov 1:7, etc.; Becker 1965); *kesut-eynayim-vindication-formula* (v 16, the hapax with the three-reading dispute resolved to the Sarna/Speiser/ASV vindication-default; Westermann); and *atzar-rechem-closed-wombs-divine-sanction* (v 18, the doubled-verb *atzor atzar* and the matriarchal-barrenness pattern across Gen 11:30, 25:21, 29:31, Judg 13:2-3, 1 Sam 1:5-6; Brichto 1973 HUCA). The *wife-sister-type-scene* entry's *appliesTo* was already locked at v2.9.0 to cover the Gen 20 verses (no expansion needed). The *imri-na-achoti-at* entry's *appliesTo* already covers GEN-20:2 and GEN-20:12 (no expansion needed). One v 13 *Elohim*-with-plural-verb-agreement datum (*hit'u Elohim* — *they caused me to wander*) is documented in commentary only and flagged for future Reviewer attention on whether a project-policy entry on plural-verb cases should be settled; the *eshet-yefat-mareh* entry is not extended to Gen 20 (the chapter does not invoke the beauty-formula). The chapter is largely glossary-recycling; the lens-relevance is structural rather than philological, with the foreign-king-as-moral-voice inversion at v 4 and the dramatic-irony of v 11 doing the chapter's principal interpretive work. The two foundational firsts are the chapter's permanent contribution to the corpus. // **v2.18.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 21** — the birth of Yitzchaq, the third laughter-play, the expulsion of Hagar and Yishma'el (the second Hagar-scene, the E-document doublet of Gen 16's J-scene), and the Avimelekh covenant at Be'er Sheva. Seven new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary, and one existing entry receives a semver-major correction. The three load-bearing entries are *metzacheq-ishmael-debate*, *malakh-elohim-messenger*, and *yhwh-el-olam*. The *metzacheq-ishmael-debate* entry (claim_type=direct) covers the four parallel readings of the piel participle *metzacheq* at v 9 — Sarah seeing Yishma'el *metzacheq* and demanding his expulsion — with the readings (a) playing / sporting (Sarna, Alter, NJPS, Westermann modern-critical default); (b) playing-with-Isaac (LXX expansion, Vulgate, Peshitta, Pauline reception at Gal 4:29, NRSV); (c) mocking / abusing (ASV/KJV, drawing on the piel's harsher uses at Gen 19:14, 39:14-17, Exod 32:6, Judg 16:25); (d) rabbinic-tradition maximal (Genesis Rabbah 53:11, b. Sotah 26b, Rashi — idolatry / sexual immorality / bloodshed). The WoH translation adopts the *playing* default, following the modern critical consensus and the editorial principle of not importing rabbinic-tradition harshness on Yishma'el where the MT does not itself carry it; the translation moves from ASV's *mocking* to *playing*. The *malakh-elohim-messenger* entry (claim_type=direct) covers the first occurrence of *mal'akh Elohim* in the Hebrew Bible at v 17 — distinct from the *mal'akh YHWH* of the *malakh-yhwh-messenger* entry — with the source-critical reading of Gen 16 (J-document) vs. Gen 21:8-21 (E-document) presented as the standard modern-critical treatment (Westermann 1985, Speiser 1964, Friedman 1987, Skinner 1910) and the post-documentary critique (Van Seters 1975, Carr 1996, Schmid 1999) also presented; the cross-corpus *mal'akh Elohim* distribution (Gen 21:17, Gen 31:11, Exod 14:19, Judg 13:6, 13:9, 1 Sam 29:9, 2 Sam 14:17, 14:20, 19:28) is documented, with the theophanic-narrative register concentrated in the E-document attribution and the complimentary-king register independent of source-critical attribution. The translation preserves *messenger of Elohim* (over ASV's *angel of God*) parallel-but-distinct from *messenger of YHWH*. The *yhwh-el-olam* entry (claim_type=direct) covers the first occurrence of *El Olam* in the HB at v 33 — the fourth El-compound in the Avraham cycle (after El Elyon Gen 14, El Ro'i Gen 16, El Shaddai Gen 17), with the religious-historical reconstruction (Alt 1929 *Der Gott der Väter*, Cross 1973 *Canaanite Myth and Hebrew Epic* ch. 1, Smith 2001, Day 2000, Köckert 1988) reading the patriarchal religion as a West-Semitic El-religion locally diversified, with the Yahwistic editor harmonizing each El-compound with YHWH through the appositional construction (*YHWH El Elyon* at Gen 14:22; *YHWH El Olam* at Gen 21:33); the *olam* lexeme is read in the temporal-duration register (HALOT, BDB, TDOT, Jenni 1952-53) rather than the abstract-eternity overreading. Four further entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary: *paqach-eynayim-revelatory-perception* (v 19, the divine-eye-opening idiom; cross-corpus Num 22:31 Balaam, 2 Kgs 6:17 Elisha's servant, 2 Kgs 6:20 the Aramean army, Isa 35:5, 42:7, Ps 146:8; the well-was-always-there modern-critical default vs. the well-created-at-the-moment rabbinic-tradition reading from Gen Rab 53:14, Pseudo-Jonathan, Tanchuma); *elohim-imkha-divine-presence-formula* (v 22, the first patriarchal occurrence of the *Elohim/YHWH im-X* divine-accompaniment formula on the lips of a non-Israelite king; cross-corpus distribution to 100+ verses; the two structural functions of divine self-disclosure and external recognition; Preuss 1968 BWANT 87 the foundational monograph); *be-er-sheva-folk-etymology* (v 31, the *sheva*-as-seven / *shava*-as-oath double-etymology; the Gen 26:33 doublet's single-sense partial; the cross-corpus *Dan-to-Be'er-Sheva* boundary-formula; Tell es-Seba archaeological identification per Aharoni 1973 and Herzog 1984/2016); *eshel-tamarisk-cultic-site* (v 33, the *Tamarix aphylla* botanical identification; the three-fold HB hapax-cluster Gen 21:33, 1 Sam 22:6, 1 Sam 31:13; the deliberate-cultic-tree-planting register distinct from the pre-existing-sacred-tree register of the wider patriarchal cycle; the sacred-tree-cultic-marker pattern across the *eshel* / *elon* / *elah* / *allon* / *tomer* lexeme-cluster — Pope 1977/1985, Frevel 1995, Olyan 1988, Hadley 2000). One semver-major correction: the *malakh-yhwh-messenger* entry's *appliesTo* previously included GEN-21:17 incorrectly (the verse uses *mal'akh Elohim*, not *mal'akh YHWH*); GEN-21:17 is removed from *malakh-yhwh-messenger*'s *appliesTo* and is now governed by the new *malakh-elohim-messenger* entry. The chapter is mid-to-high lens-leverage at three verses (v 9 *metzacheq*; v 17 *mal'akh Elohim*; v 33 *El Olam* + *eshel*), but the lens stays in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (ASV-baseline at every divergence point except for the *metzacheq* → *playing* move, which is driven by the modern critical consensus rather than by lens-considerations), and the highest-leverage entries (*metzacheq-ishmael-debate*, *malakh-elohim-messenger*, *yhwh-el-olam*) are held in mainstream scholarly register — the rabbinic-tradition harshness on Yishma'el is documented and rejected on philological grounds; the source-critical reading is presented as the standard modern-critical treatment without preempting the post-documentary critique; the religious-historical El-cult reconstruction is presented as the standard modern-critical reading documented in Alt 1929 / Cross 1973 / Smith 2001 / Day 2000 / Köckert 1988 without preempting the wider patriarchal-religion historiographical debates. Project-distinctive WoH synthesis is reserved for the wiki and long-form material downstream. // **v2.19.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 22** — the *aqedah*, the binding of Yitzchaq, the structural and theological peak of the Abraham cycle and one of the most-discussed chapters in the entire Hebrew Bible. Fourteen new entries cover the chapter's keystone vocabulary, and one existing entry receives an *appliesTo* expansion (additive). The chapter is dense with reception-history (Kierkegaardian existential, Christian Hebrews 6 and 11 + sacrificial-typology, Islamic Quran 37:101-113, Jewish *Akedah* liturgical) — all noted as downstream-not-preempted at every relevant entry. The four MANDATORY MAJOR entries that carry the chapter's theological load are *nisah-divine-testing* (v 1; the testing-an-omniscient-deity crux; Spiegel 1967, Levenson 1993, Maimonides *Guide* III.24; Kierkegaard *Fear and Trembling* as downstream-reception); *doubled-name-calling-formula* + *hineni-prophetic-response-formula* (v 11; *Avraham Avraham* paired with the threefold *hineni* at vv 1, 7, 11; cross-corpus Gen 46:2 / Exod 3:4 / 1 Sam 3:10 / Isa 6:8); *ata-yadati-divine-learning-question* (v 12; the omniscience-and-test problem; Maimonides, Brueggemann, Levenson; multiple-readings parallel); and *bi-nishbati-divine-self-oath* (v 16; the first *ne'um YHWH* in canonical narrative; Hebrews 6:13-14 reception flagged-not-preempted; Cross 1973 on the divine-self-oath as theological category). Five further entries cover load-bearing chapter-keystone material: *yechid-only-one-formula* (v 2; the *yechidkha* your-only-one with the Ishmael-existence tension; cross-corpus Judg 11:34, Ps 22:21, Zech 12:10 — the latter major NT-reception); *eretz-ha-moriah-temple-mount-identification* (v 2; the Chronicler's 2 Chr 3:1 Temple-Mount identification, the Samaritan Gerizim reading, the LXX *tēn gēn tēn hypsēlēn* variant; claim_type=inferred per Editor's call given the modern-critical division); *olah-burnt-offering* (vv 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 13; the cultic-technical whole-burnt-offering term, foundational to Lev 1-7 sacrificial system; the chapter is the first narrative use before the cultic codification); *ba-yom-ha-shlishi-third-day-formula* (v 4; cross-corpus Hos 6:2 as keystone, Exod 19:11, Esth 5:1, Jonah 2:1; the NT resurrection-typology of 1 Cor 15:4 / Luke 24:7 noted as downstream-reception); *yireh-yerah-hreh-wordplay* (vv 8 + 14; the *r-'-h* root play active/passive across the chapter, the wordplay-engine of the *aqedah*; Sarna, Wenham, Alter 2004); *yhwh-yireh-place-name-temple-mount* (v 14; place-name etiology, the active/passive *yir'eh*/*yera'eh* play, 2 Chr 3:1 cross-reference). Three smaller entries cover the chapter's specialized vocabulary: *ma'akhelet-sacrificial-knife* (v 10; the rare slaughter-knife lexeme, cross-corpus Judg 19:29, Prov 30:14); *aqad-bind-aqedah-hapax* (v 9; the verb's pentateuchal-hapax status; the chapter's name-giver in rabbinic tradition); *ayil-ram-substitution-achar-echad-variant* (v 13; the substitutionary-sacrifice ram, plus the MT *achar* vs. LXX/Syriac/Samaritan *echad* textual variant resolved to MT per established WoH textual-conservative practice; the downstream Christian vicarious-atonement reception flagged-not-preempted). One existing entry expanded: *yirat-elohim-moral-restraint-motif* receives GEN-WOH-22:12 in *appliesTo* (the *yere Elohim atah* certification of Avraham at the climax of the Aqedah, structurally inverting Avraham's own a-priori judgment at Gen 20:11). The mechanical-application entries (*elohim-as-translation*, *lekh-lekha*, *yitzchaq-name-etymology-laughter-pattern*, *sa-eynekha-lift-your-eyes*, *malakh-yhwh-messenger*, *patriarchal-seed-comparison-triad*, *nivrakhu-bekha-niphal-hithpael*) all already have the Gen 22 verses in *appliesTo* from prior versions; no expansions required. The chapter is high-lens-leverage at multiple points (v 1 the testing problem; vv 11-12 the messenger-identity continuity from Gen 16; v 14 the temple-mount identification; v 16 the divine-self-oath) but the lens stays strictly in the apparatus: the translation reads as defensible scholarly English (ASV-baseline at every divergence point, with the place-name *YHWH-yir'eh* transliterated and the textual-variant at v 13 resolved to MT). The Kierkegaardian existential reading, the Christian Hebrews 11:19 + 6:13-14 + sacrificial-typology, the Islamic Quran 37:101-113 (son's identity question), and the Jewish *Akedah* liturgical reception are all noted as downstream-not-preempted at every relevant entry; project-specific WoH synthesis on the *aqedah* is reserved for the wiki and long-form material downstream. v2.20.0 (2026-05-24): added singular-they policy for tzelem-Elohim / dmut-Elohim image-and-likeness verses (Gen 1:27, 5:1-2, 9:6). Editorial revision applied to the three already-shipped chapters. The Hebrew's grammatical-plural noun with singular-verb agreement is preserved in English via singular-they ('their image' rather than 'his image'), honoring the Hebrew tension rather than choosing one side of it. // **v2.21.0 continues the patriarchal narratives into Genesis 23** — the death of Sarah, the negotiation with the *bnei Het*, and the purchase of the *me'arat ha-Makhpelah* at Hebron. The first detailed land-transaction in the Hebrew Bible and the first piece of the promised-land actually purchased and held in title by the patriarchs. The chapter is a low-density legal-narrative — most of its work is done by chapter-internal narrative scaffolding and by existing glossary entries (*eretz*, *elonei-mamre*) — but it contains six load-bearing keystone entries that the chapter alone introduces. The six new entries: *bnei-het-hittites-canaanite-anatolian-question* (claim_type=direct) — the long-standing 2nd-millennium-Anatolian vs. indigenous-Canaanite Hittites debate, with the modern critical consensus (Sarna, Westermann, Wenham, Hamilton, Speiser) on the indigenous-Canaanite reading documented; the older Lehmann 1953 Anatolian-Hittite-law-parallel thesis documented and its modern rejection (Tucker 1966, Westbrook 1971) recorded; the wider HB *Chitti/Chittim* corpus (Gen 10:15, 26-27, 36, Num 13:29, Deut 7:1, 20:17, Josh 1:4, Judg 1:26, 1 Sam 26, 2 Sam 11, 1 Kgs 10-11, 2 Kgs 7:6, Ezek 16, 2 Chr 1, 8) forward-referenced. Translation preserves *sons of Het* (literal) at the seven *bnei Het* occurrences and *the Hittite* at the v 10 *ha-Chitti* gentilic-adjective. *nasi-elohim-honorific* (claim_type=direct) — the Hittites' formal honorific address to Avraham at v 6, with four parallel readings (literal-theological, superlative-Elohim per Thomas 1953 *VT*, polytheist-context, diplomatic-formula); the WoH translation preserves the literal *a prince of Elohim* per established WoH policy on Elohim-bearing phrases; the modern critical superlative-reading documented as the dominant alternative.
